questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Cavity resonator Q factor is:
questions.solution: Cavity stores energy in confined volume. Q = 2π × stored energy / energy loss per cycle. Higher Q than open structures.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Interrupt vector table: contains:
questions.solution: IVT: array of addresses pointing to interrupt service routines. Processor jumps to corresponding address on interrupt.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Instrumentation amplifier input impedance:
questions.solution: In-amp: FET or CMOS inputs give extremely high Zin. Essential for measuring high-impedance sources without loading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=220.0kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ, Vin=2V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Line codes — HDB3:
options: High Density Bipolar 3-zeros. Substitutes long zero runs. Used in E1 (PCM)
questions.solution: HDB3: B8ZS in T1. Four-zero substitution rule. Violation pulses for DC balance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metastability:
options: FF output oscillates between 0/1. Settling time unbounded. 2-FF synchronizer reduces probability
questions.solution: Metastability: occurs when data changes in setup-hold window. MTBF from synchronizers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Routh-Hurwitz stability criterion for s³+6s²+11s+6=0:
options: All elements in first column positive → stable
questions.solution: Routh array first column: 1, 6, (66-6)/6=10, 6. All positive → stable. Roots at -1,-2,-3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Hilbert transform:
options: Shifts phase of all frequency components by ±90°

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Intrinsic impedance of free space η₀ = √(μ₀/ε₀) ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A MOSFET is preferred in digital ICs largely because of its:
options: Very high input impedance and low power
questions.solution: The insulated gate gives near-infinite input impedance and low static power.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Digital modulation: constellation distance affects:
questions.solution: Larger constellation distance → fewer bit errors for same noise. Trade-off: more distance often needs more power or bandwidth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET transconductance gm = 2I_D/(V_GS-V_TH). For I_D=1mA, V_OV=1V:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM power:
options: Constant power (independent of modulation). Carrier and sideband amplitudes change
questions.solution: FM: total power = A_c²/2 (constant). Similar to PM. Bessel function sidebands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a BJT to operate as a linear amplifier, its junctions must be biased so that the:
options: Emitter junction is reverse and collector forward biased
Emitter junction is forward and collector junction reverse biased
questions.solution: Active (amplifying) region: emitter–base forward, collector–base reverse biased.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A channel of bandwidth 3 kHz has an SNR of 30 dB. Its Shannon capacity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A photodiode:
options: Generates current when exposed to light (reverse biased)
questions.solution: Photodiode: light → electron-hole pairs → photocurrent. PIN diode: faster response. APD: internal gain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flip-flop setup time: input data must be stable:
questions.solution: Setup time: data must be stable before clock edge. Hold time: data must remain stable after clock edge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8259 PIC (Programmable Interrupt Controller):
options: 8 interrupt inputs, priority management, vectored interrupts
questions.solution: 8259: IR0-IR7. Priority: rotating or fixed. ICW1-ICW4 initialization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Skin depth δ = √(2/(ωμσ)). As frequency increases:
questions.solution: δ ∝ 1/√f. Higher frequency → current concentrated near surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Clock domain crossing (CDC):
options: Signals between different clock domains. Need synchronizer, FIFO, or handshake
questions.solution: CDC: metastability, data loss. Gray code for multi-bit (counters). 2-FF for single-bit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8085 FLAGS register bits:
options: S, Z, AC, P, O, CY (6 flags)
S, Z, AC, P, CY (5 flags)
questions.solution: 8085: Sign, Zero, Auxiliary carry, Parity, Carry. No overflow flag.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Waveguide cutoff frequency for TE₁₀ mode in rectangular waveguide (a=2.3cm, b=1.0cm):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic hazard occurs when:
options: Output changes more than once for a single input change
questions.solution: Dynamic hazard: 3+ path delays cause multiple output transitions. Fix: eliminate multiple path delays.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMA (Direct Memory Access) transfers data:
options: Between memory and I/O without CPU involvement
questions.solution: DMA: 8257/8237 controller takes bus control. Faster than programmed I/O for bulk transfers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Faraday's law:
questions.solution: Faraday: induced EMF = -dΦ/dt. Lenz's law: direction opposes change (minus sign).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 1, B = 0, C = 1. The output Y is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boolean algebra — De Morgan's theorem:
questions.solution: De Morgan: AND↔OR interchange with complement. NAND = bubbled OR. NOR = bubbled AND.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The autocorrelation function:
options: Measures similarity of signal with itself at different lags
questions.solution: R_xx(τ) = ∫x(t)x(t+τ)dt. Wiener-Khinchin: Fourier transform of autocorrelation = power spectral density.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vestigial sideband (VSB) bandwidth is:
questions.solution: VSB: transmits full one sideband + vestige of other. BW = B + vestige. TV broadcast.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 10 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Narrowband FM has:
questions.solution: NBFM: β < 0.2. WBFM: β > 10. Carson's rule: BW = 2(Δf + f_m) = 2f_m(β+1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ASK modulation:
options: Amplitude of carrier keyed on/off by digital signal
questions.solution: ASK: On-Off Keying (OOK). Simple but susceptible to noise. BPSK better BER.

questions.stem: Among amplifier classes, the highest theoretical efficiency but most distortion belongs to:
questions.solution: Class C conducts for <180°, giving high efficiency but heavy distortion (used with tuned loads).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cut-off frequency of a simple RC low-pass filter is:
questions.solution: f_c = 1/(2πRC); above it the output falls at 20 dB/decade.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wien bridge oscillator frequency:
questions.solution: Wien: bridge circuit with lead-lag network. Requires non-linear element (lamp, JFET) for amplitude stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiplexing — CDMA:
options: Code Division. PN codes. All users same frequency/time. Orthogonal codes (Walsh)
questions.solution: CDMA: spread spectrum. IS-95, 3G. Near-far problem → power control. Soft handoff.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Pulse shaping: raised cosine filter excess bandwidth α determines:
questions.solution: α = 0: sinc pulse, minimum BW, hard to realize. α = 1: double BW, easier implementation. α trades BW for realizability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sample and hold circuit:
options: Track mode (switch closed) → hold mode (switch open, cap stores voltage). Acquisition time
questions.solution: S/H: analog memory. Acquisition time: switch to tracking after hold. Droop: capacitor discharge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM transmitter has modulation index μ = 0.6. The fraction of total power in the sidebands is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: JFET transconductance curve follows:
questions.solution: JFET: transfer characteristic is square-law. I_DSS = max drain current. V_P = pinch-off.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 75 kHz and message bandwidth 15 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The op-amp CMRR is measured in:
questions.solution: CMRR = ΔV_os/ΔV_cm. Typical 80-120 dB. Higher = better rejection of common-mode noise.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Current feedback amplifier (CFA):
options: High slew rate, gain-independent bandwidth. Non-inverting input high impedance, inverting input low
questions.solution: CFA: transimpedance stage. No GBW limitation like VFA. Slew rate >1000 V/μs.

questions.stem: Switching regulators — boost converter:
options: V_out = V_in/(1-D). Step-up. D < 1 → V_out > V_in
questions.solution: Boost: V_out > V_in. Discontinuous conduction mode at light load. Right-half-plane zero.

options: Vgs < Vt to turn off. Normally on
questions.solution: Depletion: channel exists at Vgs=0. Negative Vgs depletes channel.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Folded cascode op-amp: advantage over telescopic:
questions.solution: Folded cascode: input pair folds into cascode, allows input common-mode near mid-rail. Larger output swing than telescopic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Race condition:
options: Output depends on order of signal arrival. In latches with feedback. Avoid by edge-triggering
questions.solution: Race: S=R=1 in SR latch → Q and Q' both 1. Level-triggered JK → toggle race. Master-slave avoids.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Varactor diode acts as:
questions.solution: Varactor: reverse-biased PN junction. C_j decreases with reverse voltage.

questions.stem: Transconductance gm of BJT at room temperature: gm = I_C/V_T. For I_C=1mA:

questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=100kΩ, Rin=22kΩ, Vin=2V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARM — exception vector table:
options: Reset, Undefined, SWI/SVC, Prefetch, Data, IRQ, FIQ. Vector addresses 0x00 or 0xFFFF0000
questions.solution: ARM vectors: 8 entries × 4 bytes. FIQ: fastest (banked registers). IRQ: standard interrupt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET threshold voltage V_TH for enhancement mode NMOS is:
questions.solution: Enhancement NMOS: V_TH > 0. Depletion NMOS: V_TH < 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The speed of light in free space:
questions.solution: c = 3×10⁸ m/s. In medium: v = c/√(ε_r μ_r).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse Code Modulation (PCM):
options: Sample → quantize → encode. Quantization error = ±½ LSB. SQNR = 6.02n + 1.76 dB
questions.solution: PCM: Nyquist sampling. n bits/sample. Bit rate = n·f_s. Companding: μ-law, A-law.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Modulation — need for:
options: Frequency translation, antenna size (λ/4), multiplexing, noise immunity
questions.solution: Modulation: shift baseband to carrier frequency. Practical antenna size ∝ λ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stub matching:
options: Uses shorted/open stub of adjustable length to cancel reactance
questions.solution: Stub: single (series or shunt), double, triple. Smith chart used for design. Shunt stub is common.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: PMSM field-oriented control: requires rotor position for:
questions.solution: FOC: needs θ to transform 3-phase currents to dq frame. Encoder/observer provides rotor position.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM is generally preferred over AM for high-fidelity audio because it offers:
questions.solution: FM's constant amplitude makes it far more robust against amplitude noise than AM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse width modulation (PWM):
options: Pulse width varies with modulating signal (constant amplitude)
questions.solution: PWM: duty cycle ∝ signal amplitude. Used in motor control, class D amplifiers.
